heading: Mineral leasing claims
label: 521 Mineral leasing claims
notes: Short TitleAct , which enacted this chapter, amended , The Public Health and Welfare, and enacted provisions formerly set out as a note under , is popularly known as the Multiple Mineral Development Act.Aug. 13, 1954section 1805 of Title 42section 1805 of Title 42

Transfer of FunctionsAtomic Energy Commission abolished and functions transferred by sections 5814 and 5841 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. See, also, Transfer of Functions notes set out under those sections.

Separability, , provided that:Act Aug. 13, 1954, ch. 730, § 1368 Stat. 717“If any provision of this Act [enacting this chapter], or the application of such provision to any person or circumstances, is held unconstitutional, invalid, or unenforcible [sic], the remainder of this Act or the application of such provision to persons or circumstances other than those as to which it is held unconstitutional, invalid, or unenforcible [sic], shall not be affected thereby.”

children.text: included in a permit or lease issued under the mineral leasing laws; or
covered by an application or offer for a permit or lease which had been filed under the mineral leasing laws; or
known to be valuable for minerals subject to disposition under the mineral leasing laws,

children.heading: Labor and improvements
children.label: b Labor and improvements
children.text: Labor performed or improvements made after the original location of and upon or for the benefit of any mining claim which shall be entitled to the benefits of this chapter under the provisions of subsection (a) of this section, shall be recognized as applicable to such mining claim for all purposes to the same extent as if the validity of such mining claim were in no respect dependent upon the provisions of this chapter.

children.heading: Withdrawal or reservation of lands
children.label: c Withdrawal or reservation of lands
children.text: As to any land covered by any mining claim which is entitled to the benefits of this chapter under the provisions of subsection (a) of this section, any withdrawal or reservation of lands made after the original location of such mining claim is hereby modified and amended so that the effect thereof upon such mining claim shall be the same as if such mining claim had been located upon lands of the United States which, subsequent to , and prior to the date of such withdrawal or reservation, were subject to location under the mining laws of the United States.July 31, 1939
